MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Brittany Rogers, Esq., of Law Office of Scott Righthand Tel: 415.544.0115. for plaintiff.
JUSTINE M. CASEY, Esq., of SHEPPARD, MULLIN, RICHTER & HAMPTON LLP. Tel: 714.513.5100 for defendant San Francisco Ballet Association.
Alexandria Seibert, Esq. of HASSARD BONNINGTON LLP. 415-288-9800 for defendants REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA and WINGFIELD M.D, KRISTIN (via Zoom Conference)
Lisa Madden, Esq., of LAW OFFICES OF MADDEN & LYNCH PC. Tel:415-851-5900 for defendants GIBBS M.D, RICHARD and SAN FRANCISCO FREE CLINIC (via Zoom Conference)

Hearing is held. Defendant counsel contests the tentative ruling regarding no sanctions were issued against Plaintiff or her counsel for bringing or refusing to withdraw her motion without substantial justification and defendant's request for a discovery referee referral and all defendants' counsel believe a discovery referee is necessary going forward on all discovery disputes. The matter argued and discussed Upon conclusion; the Court adopts its tentative as follows:

Plaintiff's motion to compel the deposition of Tamara Rojo and for monetary sanctions is denied as to monetary sanctions and a hearing is required if the parties have not agreed on a date for Ms. Rojo's deposition. This motion should not have been filed since all issues relating to Ms. Rojo's deposition should have been addressed on the Ballet's motion to stay Ms. Rojo's deposition. Two motions pertaining to a single deposition is a serious misuse of the court's limited resources, not to speak of the parties' own resources. The court is extremely troubled by the parties' inability to agree on a date for the deposition. The court urges the parties to give it one more try. If the parties have not agreed to a date by the time of the hearing, counsel will need to appear with their calendars and Ms. Rojo's calendar and the court will pick a date.

The parties hereby stipulate that, on July 9, 2025, in Plaintiff counsel office. Parties may appear remotely for all matters pertaining to the deposition of Ms. Rojo. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied.

Order is signed.

Defendant San Francisco Ballet Association's motion to compel further responses to requests for admission and for monetary sanctions is denied in its entirety. The sanctions requests by both sides are denied. The Ballet Association's effort to require plaintiff to provide substantive responses in accordance with its understanding of the pertinent facts is understandable, but is not supported by applicable. If Ms. Froustey has been disingenuous in her responses, the Code of Civil Procedure provides appropriate remedies, including cost of proof sanctions. Neither side's positions lacked substantial justification, precluding the award of monetary sanctions.
